title: Streptanthus howellii
taxonomy: Streptanthus howellii is a member of the Brassicaceae family, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and seed development, which are similar to those of other Brassicaceae species. Streptanthus howellii is also classified as a genus within the tribe Thelypodieae, which is a subgroup of the Brassicaceae family. The plant's classification is further supported by its genetic relationships with other species within the family. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, Streptanthus howellii shares similarities with other plants in this group, including Arabidopsis thaliana and Brassica oleracea.

intro: Streptanthus howellii, also known as Howell's jewelflower, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to California, belonging to the Brassicaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
